summary: chai-spycore mimics chai-spies with copied author identity
details: The package name 'chai-spycore' closely resembles the established 'chai-spies' chai plugin, and the package ships a near-verbatim copy of chai-spies' source — including the original author attribution 'Jake Luer <jake@alogicalparadox.com>', the same MIT copyright header, and the same plugin API surface (index.js → lib/spy.js, chai-spies.js). No install lifecycle hooks are declared (no preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare) and the reachable code performs no network I/O, child process execution, credential access, or obfuscated logic. The risk is name confusion plus unauthorized reuse of another author's identity, not an installer-side payload. Routed for human review because name-similarity judgments are inherently subjective and the impersonation of the original author's identity warrants a human decision on whether to publish an advisory.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
